verses.text: 'Preach the word; be instant in season, out of season'; reprove, rebuke, exhort. The charge. Faithful preaching is to be constant.
'Preach the word; be instant in season, out of season'; reprove, rebuke, exhort. The charge. Faithful preaching is to be constant.
The time comes when they 'will not endure sound doctrine,' but heap up teachers 'after their own lusts.' Itching ears. Men will choose flattering teachers.
The time comes when they 'will not endure sound doctrine,' but heap up teachers 'after their own lusts.' Itching ears. Men will choose flattering teachers.
Watch, endure afflictions, 'do the work of an evangelist, make full proof of thy ministry.' Full service. Spend yourself wholly in the work.
'I have fought a good fight... henceforth there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness.' Finished course. The faithful servant awaits his crown.
'I have fought a good fight... henceforth there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness.' Finished course. The faithful servant awaits his crown.
'I have fought a good fight... henceforth there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness.' Finished course. The faithful servant awaits his crown.
Come quickly; Demas hath forsaken me, 'having loved this present world'; only Luke is with me. Lonely apostle. Some fail, but the faithful remain.
Come quickly; Demas hath forsaken me, 'having loved this present world'; only Luke is with me. Lonely apostle. Some fail, but the faithful remain.
Come quickly; Demas hath forsaken me, 'having loved this present world'; only Luke is with me. Lonely apostle. Some fail, but the faithful remain.
Come quickly; Demas hath forsaken me, 'having loved this present world'; only Luke is with me. Lonely apostle. Some fail, but the faithful remain.
Bring the cloke and the books; Alexander did me much evil; the Lord reward him. Practical and patient. He leaves his wrongs to God.
Bring the cloke and the books; Alexander did me much evil; the Lord reward him. Practical and patient. He leaves his wrongs to God.
Bring the cloke and the books; Alexander did me much evil; the Lord reward him. Practical and patient. He leaves his wrongs to God.
At my first answer none stood with me, 'but the Lord stood with me'; He will preserve me to His kingdom. Never alone. The Lord upholds His servant when men forsake him.
At my first answer none stood with me, 'but the Lord stood with me'; He will preserve me to His kingdom. Never alone. The Lord upholds His servant when men forsake him.
At my first answer none stood with me, 'but the Lord stood with me'; He will preserve me to His kingdom. Never alone. The Lord upholds His servant when men forsake him.
Salute friends; 'The Lord Jesus Christ be with thy spirit. Grace be with you.' Last words. He closes with greetings and grace.
